organic.name: Neem Oil Spray
organic.dosage: 5ml neem oil per liter of water
organic.warning: Apply in early morning or evening to avoid leaf burn. Test on a few leaves first.

organic.name: Copper-Based Fungicide (Bordeaux Mixture)
organic.dosage: 1% solution (10g copper sulfate + 10g lime per liter)
organic.warning: Can cause leaf burn in hot weather. Do not apply when temperatures exceed 30°C.

organic.name: Baking Soda Solution
organic.dosage: 1 tablespoon baking soda + 1 tablespoon oil per gallon of water
organic.warning: May alter soil pH with repeated use. Rinse residue before harvest.

chemical.name: Mancozeb
chemical.warning: Wear protective gloves and mask. Do not apply within 14 days of harvest.

chemical.name: Chlorothalonil
chemical.warning: Highly toxic to fish. Keep away from water sources. 7-day pre-harvest interval.

prevention.name: Crop Rotation
prevention.dosage: Rotate with non-solanaceous crops for 2-3 years
prevention.warning: Avoid planting tomatoes, potatoes, or peppers in the same location.

prevention.name: Remove Infected Leaves
prevention.dosage: Remove and destroy affected lower leaves immediately
prevention.frequency: Check plants daily during disease season
prevention.warning: Do not compost infected material. Burn or dispose in sealed bags.

prevention.name: Proper Spacing
prevention.dosage: Maintain 60-90cm between plants
prevention.warning: Good air circulation reduces humidity and disease spread.

organic.name: Copper Hydroxide
organic.warning: Copper can accumulate in soil. Monitor application carefully.

organic.name: Trichoderma-Based Biocontrol
organic.warning: Store in cool, dry place. Less effective once disease is established.

chemical.name: Metalaxyl + Mancozeb (Ridomil Gold)
chemical.warning: Systemic fungicide. 14-day pre-harvest interval. Rotate with other fungicides.

chemical.name: Cymoxanil + Mancozeb
chemical.warning: Apply before rain. Re-apply after heavy rainfall. Wear protective equipment.

prevention.name: Use Certified Disease-Free Seeds
prevention.dosage: Purchase from reputable sources only
prevention.warning: Infected seed tubers are the primary source of infection.

prevention.name: Destroy Volunteer Plants
prevention.dosage: Remove all volunteer potato/tomato plants
prevention.warning: Volunteer plants harbor the pathogen between seasons.

prevention.name: Improve Drainage
prevention.dosage: Ensure fields drain within 24 hours after rain
prevention.warning: Standing water promotes disease development and spread.

organic.name: Neem Oil
organic.warning: Apply in cooler parts of the day to prevent leaf burn.

organic.name: Copper Fungicide
organic.warning: Excessive use can lead to copper toxicity in soil.

chemical.name: Chlorothalonil
chemical.warning: Apply before symptoms appear for best results. 7-day pre-harvest interval.

prevention.name: Mulching
prevention.dosage: Apply 5-10cm layer of organic mulch around plants
prevention.warning: Prevents soil splash which spreads the fungus to lower leaves.

prevention.name: Stake Plants
prevention.dosage: Support plants to keep foliage off ground
prevention.warning: Improves air circulation and reduces leaf wetness.

organic.name: Soil Solarization
organic.dosage: Cover moist soil with clear plastic for 4-6 weeks
organic.warning: Most effective in summer. Kills beneficial organisms too.

organic.name: Trichoderma Application
organic.dosage: 5g per liter of water, drench soil
organic.warning: Preventive only. Cannot cure infected plants.

chemical.name: Streptomycin Sulfate
chemical.dosage: 0.5-1g per liter of water as soil drench
chemical.warning: Restricted antibiotic. Check local regulations. Not curative for established infections.

prevention.name: Use Resistant Varieties
prevention.dosage: Select varieties with bacterial wilt resistance
prevention.warning: Resistance may vary by region and bacterial strain.

prevention.name: Remove Infected Plants
prevention.dosage: Immediately uproot and destroy infected plants with surrounding soil
prevention.warning: Do not compost. Bacteria survive in soil for years.

prevention.name: Clean Tools
prevention.dosage: Disinfect tools with 10% bleach solution between plants
prevention.warning: Bacteria spread easily through contaminated tools and water.

organic.name: Neem Oil (for whitefly control)
organic.warning: Controls whiteflies that spread the virus. Cannot cure infected plants.

organic.name: Yellow Sticky Traps
organic.frequency: Install at planting, replace when covered
organic.warning: Monitoring and mass trapping of whiteflies.

chemical.name: Imidacloprid (for whitefly)
chemical.warning: Systemic insecticide. Toxic to bees. Do not apply during flowering.

prevention.name: Use Virus-Free Transplants
prevention.dosage: Source seedlings from certified nurseries
prevention.warning: Inspect seedlings carefully before purchase.

prevention.name: Install Insect-Proof Nets
prevention.dosage: 50-mesh netting over nurseries and transplants
prevention.warning: Physical barrier prevents whitefly access.

prevention.name: Remove Infected Plants
prevention.dosage: Uproot and destroy infected plants immediately
prevention.warning: Infected plants serve as virus source for healthy plants.

organic.name: Copper Hydroxide
organic.warning: Start applications before symptoms appear. Copper builds up in soil.

chemical.name: Metalaxyl + Mancozeb
chemical.warning: Alternate with non-metalaxyl products to prevent resistance.

chemical.name: Dimethomorph
chemical.warning: Systemic fungicide. 14-day pre-harvest interval.

prevention.name: Plant Certified Seed Potatoes
prevention.dosage: Use only disease-free certified seeds
prevention.warning: Infected seed tubers are primary inoculum source.

prevention.name: Hill Potatoes Properly
prevention.dosage: Mound soil 15-20cm around stems
prevention.warning: Protects tubers from spore wash-down during rain.

organic.name: Copper Fungicide
organic.warning: Apply before disease appears for prevention.

chemical.name: Mancozeb
chemical.warning: Contact fungicide. Good coverage essential.

prevention.name: Balanced Fertilization
prevention.dosage: Apply recommended NPK based on soil test
prevention.warning: Stressed, nutrient-deficient plants are more susceptible.

organic.name: Proper Seed Selection
organic.dosage: Use only healthy, certified seed tubers
organic.warning: Reject any seed with soft rot or discoloration.

chemical.name: No effective chemical control
chemical.warning: Prevention is the only effective management strategy.

prevention.name: Improve Field Drainage
prevention.dosage: Ensure complete drainage of water-logged areas
prevention.warning: Wet conditions promote bacterial growth and spread.

prevention.name: Allow Seed to Heal
prevention.dosage: Cure cut seed pieces at 15-20°C for 2-3 days
prevention.warning: Unhealed cut surfaces are entry points for bacteria.

organic.name: Trichoderma viride
organic.dosage: 4g per liter of water, foliar spray
organic.warning: Apply early as prevention. Less effective on established disease.

chemical.name: Tricyclazole
chemical.frequency: At disease appearance and 10 days later
chemical.warning: Systemic fungicide. 21-day pre-harvest interval.

chemical.name: Isoprothiolane
chemical.warning: Apply at early disease stage for best results.

prevention.name: Use Resistant Varieties
prevention.dosage: Select blast-resistant rice varieties for your region
prevention.warning: Resistance may break down over time. Use multiple resistant lines.

prevention.name: Balanced Nitrogen
prevention.dosage: Split nitrogen applications, avoid excess
prevention.warning: Excess nitrogen makes plants more susceptible to blast.

organic.name: Copper Oxychloride
organic.warning: Protectant only. Cannot cure infected tissue.

chemical.name: Streptomycin + Tetracycline
chemical.frequency: At disease onset and 7 days later
chemical.warning: Antibiotic - check local regulations. Alternate with copper.

prevention.name: Use Certified Seed
prevention.dosage: Plant seed from disease-free sources
prevention.warning: Bacteria can be seed-borne.

prevention.name: Drain Fields During Storms
prevention.dosage: Lower water level before predicted storms
prevention.warning: Typhoon and strong winds spread bacteria via leaf wounds.

organic.name: Limited Organic Options
organic.dosage: Focus on resistant varieties and cultural practices
organic.warning: No highly effective organic fungicides for rust.

chemical.name: Propiconazole
chemical.frequency: At first sign of disease, repeat in 14 days if needed
chemical.warning: Systemic fungicide. Apply before 5% leaf area infected.

chemical.name: Tebuconazole
chemical.warning: 35-day pre-harvest interval. Wear protective equipment.

prevention.name: Plant Resistant Varieties
prevention.dosage: Use locally recommended rust-resistant cultivars
prevention.warning: Rust races evolve. Monitor variety performance yearly.

prevention.name: Early Sowing
prevention.dosage: Plant at recommended early dates for your region
prevention.warning: Late-sown wheat is more susceptible to rust.

organic.name: Sulfur Dust
organic.warning: Do not apply when temperature exceeds 30°C. May cause leaf burn.

organic.name: Potassium Bicarbonate
organic.warning: Most effective as preventive treatment.

chemical.name: Triadimefon
chemical.frequency: At disease onset, repeat in 14 days
chemical.warning: Systemic fungicide. Do not exceed 2 applications per season.

prevention.name: Avoid Dense Planting
prevention.dosage: Maintain recommended seed rate for your variety
prevention.warning: Dense canopy increases humidity and disease risk.

prevention.name: Destroy Crop Residue
prevention.dosage: Incorporate stubble after harvest
prevention.warning: Fungus survives on crop residue.

organic.name: Neem-Based Products
organic.warning: Primarily helps reduce severity, not eliminate disease.

chemical.name: Mancozeb
chemical.frequency: At disease onset and 10 days later
chemical.warning: Contact fungicide. Thorough coverage needed.

prevention.name: Balanced Fertilization
prevention.dosage: Apply potassium and ensure adequate nutrition
prevention.warning: Disease is associated with nutrient-stressed plants.

prevention.name: Use Healthy Seed
prevention.dosage: Treat seed with fungicide before planting
prevention.warning: Fungus is seed-borne.

organic.name: Neem Oil Spray
organic.dosage: 5ml neem oil per liter of water
organic.warning: Apply in evening to prevent leaf burn. Ensure good leaf coverage on undersides.

organic.name: Baking Soda Solution
organic.dosage: 1 tablespoon baking soda per gallon of water with a drop of liquid soap
organic.warning: May affect soil pH with repeated use.

chemical.name: Chlorothalonil
chemical.warning: Apply before symptoms for best results. 7-day pre-harvest interval. Wear protective gear.

prevention.name: Improve Ventilation
prevention.dosage: Space plants 60-90cm apart, prune lower branches
prevention.warning: High humidity and poor air circulation promote leaf mold.

prevention.name: Avoid Overhead Watering
prevention.dosage: Use drip irrigation or water at soil level
prevention.warning: Wet leaves promote fungal growth.

organic.name: Neem Oil Spray
organic.dosage: 5ml neem oil per liter of water
organic.warning: Spray undersides of leaves thoroughly. Apply in cool hours.

organic.name: Insecticidal Soap
organic.warning: Must contact mites directly to be effective. May need repeated applications.

organic.name: Water Spray
organic.dosage: Strong jet of water on leaf undersides
organic.warning: Simple but effective for early infestations. Dislodges mites and destroys webbing.

chemical.name: Abamectin
chemical.warning: Toxic to bees and aquatic organisms. 7-day pre-harvest interval. Wear gloves and mask.

prevention.name: Maintain Humidity
prevention.dosage: Mist plants regularly in dry conditions
prevention.warning: Spider mites thrive in hot, dry conditions.

prevention.name: Introduce Predatory Mites
prevention.dosage: Release Phytoseiulus persimilis, 2-5 per plant
prevention.frequency: At first sign of spider mites
prevention.warning: Biological control. Most effective in greenhouse settings.

organic.name: Copper Fungicide
organic.warning: Start at first sign of disease. May cause copper accumulation in soil.

organic.name: Neem Oil
organic.warning: Apply in evening. Helps suppress fungal spread.

chemical.name: Chlorothalonil
chemical.warning: Contact fungicide. Apply before rainy periods. 7-day pre-harvest interval.

chemical.name: Azoxystrobin
chemical.warning: Systemic fungicide. Rotate with other modes of action to prevent resistance.

prevention.name: Remove Infected Debris
prevention.dosage: Clear all fallen leaves and infected plant material
prevention.warning: Fungus survives on crop debris between seasons.

prevention.name: Crop Rotation
prevention.dosage: Avoid planting tomatoes in the same spot for 2-3 years
prevention.warning: Reduces soil-borne inoculum buildup.

organic.name: Copper Hydroxide
organic.warning: Protectant only. Cannot cure infected tissue. Copper builds up in soil.

chemical.name: Copper + Mancozeb
chemical.dosage: Copper 2g + Mancozeb 2g per liter of water
chemical.warning: Combination provides better coverage. 14-day pre-harvest interval.

chemical.name: Streptomycin
chemical.warning: Antibiotic - check local regulations. Resistance can develop quickly.

prevention.name: Use Disease-Free Seeds
prevention.dosage: Purchase certified pathogen-free seed
prevention.warning: Bacteria can be seed-borne. Hot water seed treatment (50°C for 25 min) reduces contamination.

prevention.name: Avoid Working in Wet Fields
prevention.dosage: Wait until plants are dry before pruning or harvesting
prevention.warning: Bacteria spread rapidly through water splash and contact.

organic.name: No Cure Available
organic.dosage: Remove and destroy infected plants immediately
organic.warning: No organic treatment can cure viral infections. Focus on prevention.

organic.name: Milk Spray (Preventive)
organic.warning: Some evidence of reducing viral transmission. Not a cure.

chemical.name: No Chemical Cure
chemical.warning: No chemical treatment exists for viral diseases. Prevention is the only strategy.

prevention.name: Use Resistant Varieties
prevention.dosage: Select TMV/ToMV resistant cultivars (look for 'T' or 'TMV' resistance codes)
prevention.warning: Most reliable prevention method.

prevention.name: Sanitize Hands and Tools
prevention.dosage: Wash hands with soap, dip tools in 10% bleach between plants
prevention.warning: Virus spreads easily by touch. Smokers should wash hands before handling plants (tobacco mosaic cross-infection).

prevention.name: Remove Infected Plants
prevention.dosage: Uproot and destroy (burn or seal in bags) all infected plants
prevention.warning: Do not compost. Virus can persist in dead plant material.

prevention.name: Consult Local Expert
prevention.dosage: Contact your local agricultural extension office
prevention.warning: Professional diagnosis is recommended for accurate treatment.
